pattern: On a citation list already sorted ascending, binary search locates the leftmost index where the count of papers from that point to the end is at least the citation value there, yielding the h-index in logarithmic time.
hints: Because the array is sorted, for an index i the number of papers with at least citations[i] citations is exactly n minus i.
You are looking for the smallest index i where n minus i is no greater than citations[i]; that monotone condition invites binary search.
Binary search on i: if citations[mid] is at least n minus mid move the right bound to mid, otherwise move left past mid; the answer is n minus the converged index.

edge_inputs.description: single large element gives h-index one
all values exceed n, capped at n
leading zeros then a high run

stress.description: large sorted citation list to exercise the logarithmic search at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arr = sorted(rng.randrange(0, 1001) for _ in range(n))
    return [arr]
